    }

            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            encoding = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                parts = content_type.split('charset=')
                if len(parts) > 1:
                    encoding = parts[1].split(';')[0].strip()
            
            # Check for BOM
            max_size = 1024 * 1024  # 1MB
            data = b''
            while len(data) < max_size:
                chunk = response.read(4096)
                if not chunk:
                    break
                data += chunk
            
            # Handle content encoding
            content_encoding = response.headers.get('Content-Encoding', '')
            try:
                if content_encoding == 'gzip':
                    data = gzip.decompress(data)
                elif content_encoding == 'deflate':
                    data = zlib.decompress(data)

import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (handles both attribute orders)
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if not desc_match:
            desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']',
                                  content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception:
        # Catch all exceptions to handle any fetch/parse failure
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL format first
    try:
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not all([parsed_url.scheme, parsed_url.netloc]):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL format")
    except Exception:
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL format'
        }

    try:
        # Create SSL context with proper certificate handling
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Create request with user-agent header to avoid being blocked
        request = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={
                'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
            }
        )
        
        # Fetch the content with timeout (10 seconds)
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': f'Unable to fetch page: {str(e)}'
        }
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': f'HTTP Error: {str(e.code)} {str(e.reason)}'
        }
    except UnicodeDecodeError:
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to decode page content'
        }
    except TimeoutError:
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Request timed out'
        }
    except Exception as e:
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': f'An unexpected error occurred: {str(e)}'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import ssl
import socket
import certifi
from typing import Dict
import re


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Create request with custom headers to avoid being blocked by some websites
        request = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers={
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
        })
        
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Handle different encodings
            encoding = response.headers.get('content-type', '').split('charset=')[-1] or 'utf-8'
            try:
                content = response.read().decode(encoding)
            except (UnicodeDecodeError, LookupError):
                # Fallback to utf-8 if encoding is not supported or invalid
                content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, socket.timeout, ValueError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import re
import ssl


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Set up headers to mimic a browser
        headers = {
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/58.0.3029.110 Safari/537.3'
        }
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)
        context = ssl.create_default_context()
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
from html.parser import HTMLParser


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description from a webpage URL.
    
    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.
        
    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        Returns {'title': None, 'description': None} in case of any error.
    """
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url) as response:
            # Get the content type and extract charset
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type')
            charset = 'utf-8'  # default charset
            
            if content_type:
                parts = content_type.split(';')
                for part in parts:
                    part = part.strip().lower()
                    if part.startswith('charset='):
                        # Extract the value after the '='
                        value = part.split('=', 1)[1].strip().strip('"\'')
                        if value:  # Ensure charset value is not empty
                            charset = value
                            break
            
            # Read and decode the content
            html_content = response.read().decode(charset, errors='ignore')
        
        # Parse the HTML content
        parser = MyHTMLParser()
        parser.feed(html_content)
        parser.close()  # Ensure any pending title data is processed
        
        return {
            'title': parser.title,
            'description': parser.description
        }
        
    except Exception:
        # Return None values for both fields in case of any error
        return {'title': None, 'description': None}


class MyHTMLParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    Custom HTML parser to extract page title and description.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = None
        self.description = None
        self.in_title = False
        self.title_data = []
    
    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        """
        Handle opening tags. Specifically looks for:
        - <title> tag to start collecting title data
        - <meta> tags with name="description" to extract description
        """
        if tag == 'title':
            self.in_title = True
        elif tag == 'meta':
            # Convert attrs to a dictionary for easier access
            attr_dict = {}
            for name, value in attrs:
                if value is None:
                    value = ''
                attr_dict[name] = value
            
            # Check for description meta tag
            if ('name' in attr_dict and 
                attr_dict['name'].lower() == 'description' and
                'content' in attr_dict and 
                self.description is None):
                self.description = attr_dict['content']
    
    def handle_data(self, data):
        """
        Handle text data between tags. Collects data when inside <title> tag.
        """
        if self.in_title:
            self.title_data.append(data)
    
    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        """
        Handle closing tags. Specifically looks for </title> to finalize the title.
        """
        if tag == 'title':
            self.in_title = False
            # Combine all title data and strip whitespace
            self.title = ''.join(self.title_data).strip()
            self.title_data = []  # Reset for potential future use
    
    def close(self):
        """
        Finalize parsing. If we're still inside a <title> tag when parsing ends,
        process the collected title data.
        """
        if self.in_title:
            self.title = ''.join(self.title_data).strip()
        super().close()

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}

    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Get encoding from Content-Type header or default to utf-8
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset()
            if charset is None:
                charset = 'utf-8'
            content = response.read().decode(charset)

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'\s*<title>\s*(.*?)\s*</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        patterns = [
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
            r'<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']'

            if desc_match:
                description = desc_match.group(1).strip()
                break

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, UnicodeDecodeError, socket.timeout):

import urllib.request
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the HTML content of a given URL and extracts the title and description metadata.

    Args:
        page_url (str): The URL of the webpage to fetch.

    Returns:
        dict: A dictionary containing the title and description of the webpage.
              If an error occurs, the dictionary will contain an 'error' key with the error message.
    """
    # Create a secure SSL context using certifi
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            html_content = response.read().decode('utf-8')
    except Exception as e:
        return {'error': str(e)}
    
    class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
        def __init__(self):
            super().__init__()
            self.title = None
            self.description = None
            self.in_head = False
            self.current_tag = None
            self.done = False
        
        def _set_done(self):
            self.done = True
        
        def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
            if self.done:
                return

            if tag == 'head':
                self.in_head = True
            elif tag == 'body':
                self.in_head = False
                self._set_done()

            if not self.in_head:
                return

            if tag == 'title':
                self.current_tag = 'title'
            elif tag == 'meta':
                attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
                if 'content' in attrs_dict:
                    name = attrs_dict.get('name', '').lower()
                    property_val = attrs_dict.get('property', '').lower()
                    if name == 'description' or property_val == 'og:description':
                        self.description = attrs_dict['content']
                        if self.title is not None:
                            self._set_done()
        
        def handle_data(self, data):
            if self.done or self.current_tag != 'title':
                return
            if self.title is None:
                self.title = data.strip()
            else:
                self.title += data.strip()
            if self.description is not None:
                self._set_done()
        
        def handle_endtag(self, tag):
            if self.done:
                return
            if tag == 'head':
                self.in_head = False
                self._set_done()
            elif tag == 'title':
                self.current_tag = None
                if self.description is not None:
                    self._set_done()
    
    parser = MetadataParser()
    try:
        parser.feed(html_content)
    except Exception:
        # Ignore parsing errors and return whatever was collected
        pass
    
    return {
        'title': parser.title,
        'description': parser.description
    }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }
    except Exception:
        # Catch any other unexpected exceptions to prevent crashes
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme
        parsed = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
            raise urllib.error.URLError("Only HTTP and HTTPS schemes are supported")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with timeout
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from typing import Dict
import ssl
import certifi
import re


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            raw_content = response.read()
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset() or 'utf-8'
        
        try:
            content = raw_content.decode(charset)
        except (UnicodeDecodeError, LookupError):
            try:
                content = raw_content.decode('utf-8')
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                content = raw_content.decode('latin-1')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description with improved pattern
        pattern = r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']|' \
                  r'<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']'
        desc_match = re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if desc_match:
            description = desc_match.group(1) if desc_match.group(1) else desc_match.group(2)
            description = description.strip()
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception:
        # If any error occurs during the process, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    HTML parser to extract title and description metadata from web pages.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title: Optional[str] = None
        self.description: Optional[str] = None
        self.in_title_tag: bool = False
        self.title_data: list = []
    
    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list) -> None:
        """Handle opening tags to identify title and description elements."""
        if tag == 'title':
            self.in_title_tag = True
        elif tag == 'meta':
            # Look for description meta tag (case-insensitive)
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            name = attrs_dict.get('name', '').lower()
            if name == 'description':
                self.description = attrs_dict.get('content', '')
    
    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str) -> None:
        """Handle closing tags to finalize title extraction."""
        if tag == 'title':
            self.in_title_tag = False
            # Join all title data and strip whitespace
            self.title = ''.join(self.title_data).strip()
            self.title_data = []
    
    def handle_data(self, data: str) -> None:
        """Handle text content within tags."""
        if self.in_title_tag:
            self.title_data.append(data)


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> Dict[str, Optional[str]]:
    """
    Fetch and extract metadata (title and description) from a web page.
    
    Args:
        page_url: The URL of the web page to fetch metadata from.
        
    Returns:
        Dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' keys with extracted metadata.
        Returns None values for keys if metadata is not found or if fetching fails.
    """
    # Validate URL format before making request
    if not page_url or not isinstance(page_url, str):
        return {'title': None, 'description': None}
    
    # Create SSL context with certifi for secure connections
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    try:
        # Make HTTP request with secure SSL context
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Read and decode the HTML content
            html_content = response.read()
            encoding = response.headers.get_content_charset('utf-8')
            html_text = html_content.decode(encoding or 'utf-8', errors='replace')
            
            # Parse HTML to extract metadata
            parser = MetadataParser()
            parser.feed(html_text)
            
            # Unescape HTML entities and clean up metadata
            title = parser.title
            description = parser.description
            
            if title:
                title = html.unescape(title.strip())
            if description:
                description = html.unescape(description.strip())
                
            return {
                'title': title,
                'description': description
            }
            
    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        # Handle URL-specific errors (404, connection issues, etc.)
        return {'title': None, 'description': None}
    except Exception as e:
        # Handle any other unexpected errors
        return {'title': None, 'description': None}
